card_json.name: Devoted Sentinel
card_json.flavor_text: I stand watch so that others may rest in peace.
card_json.design_notes: A simple white common creature with vigilance, fitting white's color pie perfectly. At 2 mana for a 2/1 with vigilance, it follows the power level guidelines for commons (P+T = 3, which equals CMC + 1, appropriate for a relevant keyword ability). Vigilance is an evergreen keyword that's easy to understand and fits white's defensive nature. The Human Soldier typing is flavorful and mechanically relevant for tribal synergies.

card_json.name: Temporal Insight
card_json.oracle_text: Look at the top four cards of your library. Put two of them into your hand and the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.\nDraw a card.
card_json.flavor_text: "The threads of possibility reveal themselves to those who know how to look." —Jace Beleren
card_json.design_notes: This card provides card selection and advantage at uncommon complexity. The effect lets you see 4 cards, keep the 2 best, and still draws you a card for net +1 card advantage. At 3 mana, this is appropriately costed for blue's card draw and selection abilities. The "bottom of library in any order" gives additional control. This fits blue's color pie perfectly - card draw, library manipulation, and no direct interaction with the battlefield. The complexity is moderate with two connected effects, appropriate for uncommon.

prompt_text: Generate a blue uncommon instant spell. CMC 2-4. Can have a moderately complex effect. Include flavor text.

card_json.name: Vorthak, Death's Whisper
card_json.oracle_text: Deathtouch\nWhenever a creature an opponent controls dies, you may pay {1}{B}. If you do, return target creature card from your graveyard to your hand.\n{3}{B}{B}, {T}: Destroy target creature. Its controller loses life equal to its mana value.
card_json.flavor_text: Death is not an ending, but a transaction. I merely negotiate the terms.
card_json.design_notes: This legendary demon warlock embodies black's core themes of death, sacrifice, and reanimation. The triggered ability creates value from opponents' creatures dying (very black), while the activated ability provides expensive but powerful removal with life loss punishment. The 3/4 deathtouch body is reasonable for 4 mana but not overpowered. The design encourages building around creature death triggers and graveyard value, making it a solid rare build-around commander option. The mana costs prevent it from being oppressive in faster formats while rewarding dedicated black decks.

card_json.name: Kythara, Seer of Storms
card_json.oracle_text:
+1: Scry 2, then draw a card. ~ deals 1 damage to any target.
−3: Exile the top four cards of your library. Until end of turn, you may cast instant and sorcery cards from among them. Spells cast this way cost {2} less to cast.
−7: You get an emblem with "Whenever you cast an instant or sorcery spell, copy it. You may choose new targets for the copy."
card_json.flavor_text: She reads the future in lightning's path and thunder's echo.
card_json.design_notes: This planeswalker embodies the blue-red identity of instant/sorcery synergy and impulsive spellcasting. The +1 provides incremental value through scry and card draw (blue) plus direct damage (red), making it immediately impactful. The -3 enables explosive turns by giving access to multiple spells at a discount, representing red's impulsive nature and blue's spell mastery. The ultimate creates a game-ending emblem that doubles spell effects. At 4 CMC with 4 starting loyalty, it's competitively costed but requires protection. The abilities scale appropriately for mythic complexity while staying true to both colors' strengths.

card_json.name: Moonlit Observatory
card_json.oracle_text: ~ enters tapped unless you control two or more other lands.\n{T}: Add {W} or {U}.\n{2}{W}{U}, {T}: Scry 2, then draw a card. Activate only as a sorcery.
card_json.flavor_text: The stars whisper secrets to those patient enough to listen.
card_json.design_notes: This uncommon dual land provides White/Blue mana with an activated ability that fits both colors' identities - scry (blue) and card draw (blue) with white in the cost for balance. The enters-tapped condition unless you control two other lands encourages slower, more controlling decks typical of W/U strategies. The sorcery-speed restriction prevents it from being too powerful as an instant-speed effect. The mana cost of {2}{W}{U} makes it a significant investment but provides real card advantage for control decks.
